What the mechanic does: Drift is about INPUTS, not outputs. At station lock the engine hashes each completed unit's declared input files — the premises it signed against. Each tick the sweep re-hashes them; a premise that moved (upstream, or an out-of-band human/agent hand-edit) means the work may need to re-orient, so the engine restamps the witness (fires once), re-anchors annotations, and files ONE origin=drift FEEDBACK against the affected unit's station. Outputs are NOT witnessed (they're the mutable product; witnessing them loops). A file that is both an input and a same-station output is baton-exempt. The agent then classifies cosmetic vs material; material invalidates the unit's signed slots → re-sign re-orients. This is how human-in-the-loop changes get taken into regard as the run iterates.
Predecessor behavior: witnesses input files; input change → feedback; baton exemption
darkrun detail: drift.rs: inputs-only witness, produced-set baton exemption, restamp-on-detect → origin=drift feedback (dedup by premise+kind, cascade-capped); feedback set_targets → invalidates re-opens stamps; Track C retired
Rationale / next step: REBUILT this session against the predecessor source. The prior pass was wrong: it witnessed outputs (loop-prone), had no baton exemption (in-place-edit stations self-drifted), and used a global Track-C hold instead of scoped feedback.

What the mechanic does: On detecting drift the engine restamps the witness to the current hash BEFORE filing, so the same change fires exactly once and the feedback (not a re-firing hold) owns the unresolved re-orientation. The premise that moved is captured as the new baseline; the feedback carries the decision.
Predecessor behavior: restamp before filing so it can't re-fire
darkrun detail: drift.rs sweep restamps before filing the drift feedback
Rationale / next step: Now implemented as part of the B1 rebuild (was previously mis-filed 'keep'). The predecessor restamps-on-detect; darkrun now does too, with the feedback as the durable signal. accept() remains for an explicit operator rebaseline.

What the mechanic does: The same drifted artifact files exactly one drift entry rather than one per sweep — the drift id is keyed per artifact (and per unit+input for premises), so re-sweeps overwrite rather than pile up.
darkrun detail: drift.rs: one open origin=drift feedback per premise+kind (marker-deduped)
Rationale / next step: Shipped; now dedupes the drift FEEDBACK (one per premise+kind) per the rebuild.

What the mechanic does: Caps how many open drift entries a sweep will file (default 10, env-tunable) so one widely-consumed artifact moving — or a directory rename — can't bury the run in drift the operator can't act on.
darkrun detail: drift.rs cascade_cap / DARKRUN_DRIFT_CASCADE_CAP — bounds open drift feedback; restamp still runs
Rationale / next step: Shipped; now caps open drift FEEDBACK (restamp continues past the cap) per the rebuild.

Rationale / next step: Shipped. Bound the nonce to the recorded quality-gate result — an agent can't certify a gate it wasn't dispatched to run.

What the mechanic does: A fix-worker repairs code routed back from a checkpoint, and each unit runs its Pass-loop — both now on their own branch + worktree forked off the station branch, isolating one diff at a time and landing it back on resolution/lock (the same fork/isolate/land discipline a station has against run-main). Gives clean retry, a conflict-pause, and a guarded merge-back per unit and per fix.
Predecessor behavior: fix-chain on isolated branch; merge-back on close
darkrun detail: lifecycle.rs enter_unit/land_unit + enter_fix/land_fix (parallel units/ & fixes/ namespaces); cursor forks each wave unit on Manufacture + lands on Audit, forks each fix on FixFeedback/ResolveDrift + lands on close/accept; Unit.branch stamped; manufacture/fix prompts point the worker at its worktree
Rationale / next step: Shipped BOTH per-unit AND per-fix isolation this session (your call). Verified end-to-end on git-backed runs.

What the mechanic does: The run mode (continuous / discrete / autopilot / quick) shapes which review/approval roles fire — at both station and run level — while always keeping the final operator gate. darkrun shapes station-level roles only.

What the mechanic does: Turn a human's review on the discrete-mode draft PR/MR (comments + change-requests) back into darkrun feedback the fix track addresses — the PR becomes a two-way bridge, not just a merge-state poll. The discrete poll fetches the PR's review notes each tick and files each new one as external-origin feedback; the open feedback preempts the held external gate, gets worked + closed, and the fix is re-pushed to the PR.
